item.block1: The MARPOL Convention requires that every oil tanker of 150 tons gross tonnage and above
item.block2: carries on board a shipboard oil pollution emergency plan.

item.block1: A shipboard marine pollution emergency plan for noxious liquid substances
item.block2: should be combined with a shipboard oil pollution emergency plan.

item.block1: One combined plan on board is more practical than
item.block2: two separate plans in the event of an emergency.

item.block1: The title of such a combined plan should be ‘Shipboard marine pollution emergency plan’
item.block2: in order to distinguish it from the other two emergency plans mentioned above.

item.block1: Regulations of Annex I and Annex II of MARPOL
item.block2: prescribe that the plans have to be in accordance with the guidelines of the IMO.

item.block1: It is relevant to note that shipboard oil pollution emergency plans which have already been approved

item.block2: the development of shipboard oil pollution emergency plans.

item.block1: The main objectives of these guidelines are to assist shipowners
item.block2: in preparing shipboard marine pollution emergency plans.

item.block1: The main objectives of these guidelines are to assist governments
In the interest of uniformity, governments
item.block2: are requested to refer to these guidelines when preparing appropriate national regulations.
